report_name: Booking.com Warns Customers of Data Breach Exposing Reservation Details and Personal Info

threat_actors.name: Unknown Actors
threat_actors.description: Unauthorized third parties who conducted a supply chain attack by compromising Booking.com hotel partner accounts, likely via phishing, to access and collect customer reservation data for future phishing campaigns.
threat_actors.goals: Collect personal and reservation data to conduct highly targeted phishing attacks for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained indirect access to Booking.com's data by compromising the accounts of trusted hotel partners in a supply chain attack.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used legitimate, stolen credentials of hotel partners to log into the Booking.com partner portal, making their activity difficult to distinguish from normal operations.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: Once authenticated to the partner portal, the attackers collected reservation data for all upcoming bookings at the compromised hotels.

identities.name: Booking.com
identities.description: A leading online travel agency whose platform was abused to access customer reservation data via compromised partner accounts.

identities.name: Hotel Partners
identities.description: Accommodation providers partnered with Booking.com whose administrative accounts on the partner portal were compromised, leading to the data breach.

identities.name: Booking.com Customers
identities.description: Customers of Booking.com whose personal and reservation data were accessed by unauthorized parties, exposing them to targeted phishing attacks.
